title: YC demo video shows the full Codex-to-Kage memory loop
summary: The YC application demo should be a 72-second silent 16:9 MP4 showing the full product loop: Codex calls Kage context, recalls repo memory, uses code graph facts, captures a repo-local memory packet, refreshes the graph, and shows inspectable memory in the viewer.
body: The YC application demo should show Kage as an end-to-end memory harness rather than only a viewer tour. The rendered asset is docs/assets/kage-yc-demo.mp4 with poster docs/assets/kage-yc-demo-poster.png, generated by scripts/render-yc-demo.mjs. The story is: every agent starts cold; Codex calls kage_context; Kage returns relevant repo memory; source-derived code graph facts guide implementation; kage_capture writes repo-local memory; kage_refresh updates graph/metrics; viewer-style graph proves memory links back to source paths and evidence. Keep it under YC's 3 minute / 100 MB demo limit.

context.fact: The YC demo video should show the full Codex-to-Kage memory loop, not only a viewer tour.
context.why: The strongest product proof is an end-to-end agent flow: recall, code graph guidance, capture, refresh, and inspectable evidence.
context.trigger: Recall when editing YC demo scripts, rendered video assets, posters, or product demo story.
context.action: Keep scripts/render-yc-demo.mjs focused on the complete memory-harness loop and keep the MP4 under YC limits.
context.verification: Repo-local capture grounded to scripts/render-yc-demo.mjs, docs/assets/kage-yc-demo.mp4, and docs/assets/kage-yc-demo-poster.png.
context.risk_if_forgotten: Agents may produce a shallow viewer-only demo that fails to prove the core memory handoff loop.
context.stale_when: YC demo requirements change or a newer canonical application demo supersedes these assets.
